metaDescription: See examples of how businesses structure their Freshdesk knowledge base to help customers self-serve answers quickly.
intro: A well-organized knowledge base reduces ticket volume by letting customers find answers themselves. This guide looks at examples of how Freshdesk knowledge bases can be structured for clarity and easy navigation.

steps.heading: Organize by category
steps.body: Group articles into clear categories and folders so customers can browse by topic rather than searching blindly.

steps.heading: Write focused articles
steps.body: Keep each article centered on a single question or task, making it easy to scan and act on.

steps.heading: Use consistent formatting
steps.body: Apply consistent headings, screenshots, and step numbering across articles so the knowledge base feels cohesive.

steps.heading: Highlight popular articles
steps.body: Surface your most-viewed or most-helpful articles prominently so common questions get answered fastest.

faq.question: How many categories should a knowledge base have?
faq.answer: It depends on your product complexity, but most effective knowledge bases keep categories broad enough to be scannable, not overly granular.

faq.question: Can I see which articles are most viewed?
faq.answer: Yes, Freshdesk provides analytics showing which knowledge base articles get the most traffic and engagement.

faq.question: Should I include screenshots in articles?
faq.answer: Yes, screenshots and visuals generally make step-by-step articles easier to follow.

faq.question: Can I restrict some knowledge base articles to certain users?
faq.answer: Yes, Freshdesk allows you to set visibility so some articles are public while others are internal only.
